title: Boundaryless Existence
definition: The recognition that one is an integral part of a web of relationships and interconnected systems, blurring the boundaries between self and environment.

proofs.description: Extracted from conversation via CADMIES Harvest Pipeline
proofs.reference: CADMIES Harvest Pipeline v4.0 — raw dump rescue

difficulty_levels.beginner: We are all connected to each other and to the world around us.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The recognition that one is an integral part of a web of relationships and interconnected systems, blurring the boundaries between self and environment.
difficulty_levels.expert: Boundaryless existence posits that the distinction between self and environment is a perceptual construct. Every thought, word, and action propagates through interconnected systems, making each individual a constant, ongoing cause in the broader web of existence.

extra_fields.insight: Every thought, word, and action has an effect on the world around us, making us constant, ongoing causes in the system.
extra_fields.historical_context: Emerged within contemporary ecological philosophy and discussions on the interconnectedness of all life.
limitations: May oversimplify the complexities of individuality and personal identity
Assumptions about interconnectedness may not apply to all cultural or philosophical traditions.
